reasoning: According to the Wikipedia 'FIDE rankings' page as of 2025-03-02, Shakhriyar Mamedyarov's rating is 2748. For the criteria to resolve YES, his rating must be at least 1% higher than this baseline, which corresponds to 2748 * 1.01 = 2775.48 (effectively requiring an Elo rating of 2776 or higher). FIDE publishes its official rating lists on the 1st of each month. Therefore, on 2025-03-09, the published rating will almost certainly still be the March rating of 2748, maintaining an identical value and ensuring a near-zero probability. To reach 2776 over the ensuing months, Mamedyarov would need to gain 28 Elo points. At the super-GM level, a 28-point surge requires a massively dominating performance in top-tier (2700+) tournaments over an extended period. Given that he is 39 years old and has not consistently held a 2770+ rating for several years, achieving this milestone is theoretically possible but practically very unlikely. I assign base minimum probabilities in the short term, scaling up slightly over the one-year horizon to account for increased variance and the possibility of a late-career hot streak.
